footnotes.text: Charity: here is meant the supernatural virtue comprising love of God above all things and love of neighbor for God. The Greek word implies, not a mere sentimental feeling, but a sense of appreciation of the worth of God in Himself and in one's neighbor.

Charity is the will's choice of God based on the knowledge of God. We know Him now indirectly through his reflection in creatures. This indirect vision is vague and distorted as was the vision seen through the metal mirrors of St. Paul's day. But when grace becomes the beatific vision through the lifting of the veil, we shall see God directly and know Him as He knows us.
